summary: First-finish smoke test for AI Quest. Backend curriculum & lesson endpoints work. Full end-to-end lesson flow verified: Home -> Start Lesson -> teach cards + 6 exercises -> Completion screen -> XP earned -> persisted in localStorage on reload -> next lesson unlocked. Profile and Path pages render correctly. Monochrome design intact. One minor non-blocking UI issue: the floating 'Made with Emergent' badge overlays the bottom-nav 'Profile' button area, intercepting clicks via standard click (force=True works).
passed: GET /api/curriculum returns 200 with correct units/lessons structure
Home page renders with title, streak-count, xp-count, todays-lesson-card
Navigation to /path works; shows 3 lessons with L1 unlocked, L2/L3 locked
Start Lesson navigates to /lesson/u0-l1 and lesson player loads
Teach cards display, exercises (multiple_choice/true_false/fill_blank) render with options
Answer selection enables Next button; explanation panel appears (Correct!/Not quite)
Lesson completion screen shows with 'Lesson Complete!' title and XP earned
XP accrued to home (0 -> 4 XP after one run) and PERSISTED across page reload
Progress card updated to 1/3 lessons complete after L1 completion
Lesson 2 (AI vs ML) becomes unlocked on Path; Lesson 3 remains locked
Profile page shows Day Streak 1, Total XP 4, Lessons 1 and reset-progress-button present
data-testid attributes present on all key interactive elements
Framer Motion transitions render without visible errors

failed.issue: 'Made with Emergent' floating badge overlaps the bottom nav (especially the Profile button) and intercepts pointer events; standard Playwright click hits the badge link instead of nav button. Real users on small mobile widths may experience similar tap-target overlap.
failed.evidence: Playwright error: '<a id="emergent-badge"> intercepts pointer events' when clicking [data-testid="nav-profile"]. Direct URL navigation and force-click both work.

action_items: Optional: adjust bottom nav z-index or shift Emergent badge so it does not overlap the Profile nav tap target on 390px wide viewports.
